title: Phaeneilema glaucum
taxonomy: Phaeneilema glaucum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acrocerotaceae, which is a family of epiphytic plants. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The family Acrocerotaceae is a relatively small family within the order Lamiales, and is native to tropical regions. Phaeneilema glaucum is an epiphytic plant, which means it grows on other plants, typically trees, for support. The exact classification of Phaeneilema glaucum may be subject to change as new research becomes available.
uses: Phaeneilema glaucum is reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial in treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
